question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a hex-encoded EVM address shorter than 12 bytes (e.g., '0x1234') so that ByteBuffer.wrap(evmAddressBytes) creates a buffer too small for buffer.getInt(), triggering an unhandled BufferUnderflowException that crashes the GraphQL service thread and prevents new transaction confirmations?
